content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mae/.claude/skills/assignment-design

# /assignment-design — 사용자 주도 설계 Q&A

코코 = 진행자, 사용자 = 결정자. **`_shared/`가 콘텐츠의 정본**. SKILL은 그 정본을 단계마다 사용자한테 풀어주고, 사용자가 결정하게 만드는 conductor.

---

## 정본 위치 (모두 절대 경로 — 항상 여기서 읽음)

| 파일 | 역할 | 언제 읽음 |
|---|---|---|
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md`** | 정본 — JD signal map, 5 design principles, Phase 0–6 timeline, Agent A/B prompts, 11 anti-patterns | **Phase A에서 무조건** |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/LESSONS_LEARNED.md`** | 직전 세션의 실수와 교훈 | **Phase A에서 같이** — 같은 실수 반복 방지 |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/agent-architecture.md`** | Anthropic 기반 agent 설계 결정 가이드 (Workflow vs Agent, context engineering 등) | Phase F (5 Design Principles) |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/README_5sections.md`** | README 5섹션 영어 템플릿 | Phase C (README skeleton) + Phase H (final prompt) |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/AGENTS.md`** | Engineering Manifesto (이 repo의) | Phase B (constraint baseline) |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/agents/builder.md`** | Builder sub-agent 정의 | Phase H — Codex 프롬프트의 builder 역할 |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/agents/reviewer.md`** | Reviewer sub-agent 정의 | Phase H — diff 검토 단계 |
| **`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/agents/tester.md`** | Tester sub-agent 정의 | Phase H — 테스트 작성 단계 |
| **`~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md`** | FastAPI stack constraint (auto-loaded if FastAPI 탐지) | Phase A |

**규칙**: 위 파일들은 **항상 절대 경로로** Read. cwd에 의존 X. 변경 가능성 있는 정본이라 매번 새로 읽음 (캐시 X).

---

## 작동 규칙

1. **Phase A에서 PROTOCOL.md + LESSONS_LEARNED.md를 먼저 Read**. 못 찾으면 즉시 stop + 사용자한테 알림.
2. **`_shared/`의 모든 default 답이 정본**. 코코는 *"이대로 갈래? 다른 거?"* 식으로 제시.
3. **한 턴에 결정 1개**. 옵션 2~3개 + trade-off + protocol default 추천 + 본인 결정.
4. **사용자가 "다 알아서 해줘" 하면 거부**: *"protocol이 default를 갖고 있어. 사용자가 confirm 하거나 override 해야 평가 가능."*
5. **매 결정 즉시 `SPEC.md` (현재 프로젝트 cwd 기준)에 append**. 첫 write 전에 `pwd`로 절대 경로 확인.
6. **PROTOCOL의 mandate 항목** (deterministic-sandwich, 3-tier schema, brand field, trace_id, tool_use)을 사용자가 빼려 하면 거부 + JD signal 손실 경고.
7. **절대 코드 X**. PROTOCOL의 README skeleton, Pydantic 모델 stub, Agent prompts 그대로 인용/customize만.

---

## 톤

한국어. 5문장 이내 per turn. 능동태. 호칭: 코코.

---

## Phase A — Load (자동, 30초)

코코가 자동으로:

```
1. Read /Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md
2. Read /Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/LESSONS_LEARNED.md
3. Stack 탐지 (assignment 본문에서 키워드 추출)
4. FastAPI stack이면 Read ~/.claude/commands/fastapi.md
```

사용자에게 알림:
```
✅ PROTOCOL.md loaded — Phase 0–6, JD signal map (11), 5 principles, Agent A/B prompts, 11 anti-patterns
✅ LESSONS_LEARNED.md loaded — N개 실수 (직전 세션) — 같은 실수 안 하게 진행
✅ Stack detected: <FastAPI / Next.js / Go / unknown>
✅ Stack constraints: <fastapi.md 로드 여부>

직전 실수 중 이번 assignment에 적용될 것 (LESSONS_LEARNED에서 추출):
- <Mistake X>: <next-time action>
- <Mistake Y>: <next-time action>
이 실수들 의식하면서 진행.
```

→ 사용자한테 한 줄:
```
Spec 어디 있어? (붙여넣기 / 파일 경로) + 시간 예산 (90분 / 4h)?
```

---

## Phase B — PROTOCOL §Phase 0 가이드 (XY-30S + Risk + P0/P1/P2)

PROTOCOL의 §Phase 0 (3 sub-step)을 사용자한테 던져서 confirm/조정.

### B.1 — XY-30S 4 bullets
PROTOCOL §0.1의 4 bullet 그대로 customize해서 보여줌:
```
- WHO: <protocol's WHO, customized to user's assignment>
- WHAT friction: ...
- NON-DETERMINISTIC: ...
- EXTERNAL: ...

이대로 너 assignment에 맞아? 수정?
```
→ 사용자 confirm/수정. `SPEC.md`의 `## Problem`.

### B.2 — Risk Inventory
PROTOCOL §0.2의 5개 risk + LESSONS_LEARNED의 직전 risk 그대로 보여주고:
```
이 5+N개 risk 중 너 assignment에 해당 안 되는 거 있어? 추가할 risk 있어?
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## Risks`.

### B.3 — P0/P1/P2 Cut List
PROTOCOL §0.3의 3-tier cut + 시간 예산 매핑:
```
P0 (0–<X>min): <protocol default>
P1 (<X>–<Y>min): <protocol default>
P2 (README mention only): <protocol default>

너 시간 예산 + assignment에 맞나? 빠진 거? P1 → P0 올릴 거?
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## Scope`.

---

## Phase C — PROTOCOL §Phase 1 가이드 (README skeleton + Schemas)

### C.1 — README §1–§5 골격
**먼저 Read `/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/README_5sections.md`** — 정본 템플릿.
PROTOCOL §Phase 1.1의 README 골격과 합쳐서 customize:
```
README §1 (Problem & Approach):
  PROTOCOL의 default 6개 Assumption + 3개 Ambiguity 보여줄게.
  너 assignment에 안 맞는 거 / 추가할 거?
README §2 (Domain Model):
  PROTOCOL의 entity 4개 (Session/PlanStep/PatchProposal/GuardrailCheck) — 너 assignment의 entity와 매핑돼?
README §3 (AI Leverage):
  AI/Hand 분리 안 함 — Component | Done by | Verification 표 (PROTOCOL §Phase 1.1 그대로)
README §4 (Trade-offs):
  PROTOCOL의 default 6개 trade-off — 너 assignment에 추가할 거?
README §5 (If More Time):
  PROTOCOL의 default 6개 — JD signal 직격 (OTEL, multi-brand, SKILL Skills, SQLite, LLM-as-judge, Evaluator-Optimizer)
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## README Plan`.

### C.2 — Pydantic 3-tier Schemas
PROTOCOL §Phase 1.2의 3-tier (LLM Input / Out / Domain) + brand·trace_id·explainability 박힌 stub 그대로 보여주고:
```
이 schema가 너 assignment의 entity와 매핑돼?
- Entity 이름 다르면 substitute 가능
- 하지만 다음은 PROTOCOL mandate (못 빼):
  - 3-tier 분리 (SRP)
  - brand 필드 (JD signal: multi-brand)
  - trace_id (JD signal: OTEL)
  - reason: str (Explainability)
- 다른 entity 추가할 거 있나?
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## Domain Model`.

---

## Phase D — PROTOCOL §Phase 2 가이드 (Architecture Diagram)

PROTOCOL §Phase 2의 deterministic-sandwich 다이어그램 + 2개 signal sentence 보여줌:
```
Architecture (deterministic sandwich):
[디아그램 verbatim from PROTOCOL §Phase 2]

Opening sentence (README §1 verbatim):
"This service converts implicit human debugging workflows into explicit, observable agent workflows — the LLM proposes, deterministic checks decide."

Positioning sentence (README §1 2nd line):
"It is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer that injects brand context (AGENTS.md) and enforces Engineering Manifesto guardrails before any AI-generated patch can be merged."

너 assignment 흐름과 일치해? Endpoint 이름 다르면 mapping 알려줘.
- Sandwich 골격 (deterministic in → LLM → deterministic out)은 mandate
- 2개 signal sentence는 verbatim 추천 (수정 가능하지만 톤 깨면 알림)
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## Architecture`.

---

## Phase E — JD Signal Map 체크 (가장 중요)

PROTOCOL §JD Signal Map의 11행 표를 사용자한테 한 행씩 던짐:
```
JD signal #N — <keyword>
PROTOCOL default mapping: <where it lands in code/README>

너 assignment에서:
  1) 이 signal 그대로 적용 OK
  2) 다른 곳에 매핑 (어디?)
  3) 이 assignment엔 해당 안 됨 (이유?)

결정?
```
11행 다 끝나면 `SPEC.md`의 `## JD Signal Map` 섹션에 표로 박음.

**중요**: PROTOCOL은 *"every row above must map to ≥1 line of code or README text"* 라고 mandate. 사용자가 한 행이라도 "skip" 하면 코코가 reason 받아서 명시.

---

## Phase F — 5 Design Principles 적용 + Agent Architecture 확인

**먼저 Read `/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/agent-architecture.md`** — Anthropic 기반 결정 가이드.

PROTOCOL §Design Principles 5개 + agent-architecture의 핵심 결정을 종합해서 보여줌:
```
PROTOCOL의 5 원칙:
1) SRP — each endpoint = one state transition
2) Deterministic Boundary — LLM transport, guardrail regex
3) YAGNI — spec에 없으면 P2 README 언급
4) Workflow-first — endpoint = state machine arrow 1개씩
5) Explainability — 모든 AI output에 traceable reason

agent-architecture.md의 추가 가이드:
- Workflow vs Agent? (이 assignment는 어느 쪽?)
- Multi-agent vs single agent? (코딩 도메인은 보통 single)
- Context engineering: just-in-time? compaction?
- Long-running harness 필요?

각 원칙·결정에 대해 너 assignment 특화 1줄 박을 거야. 수정할 거?
```
→ `SPEC.md`의 `## Design Principles Applied`.

---

## Phase G — 설계 요약 + 사용자 확인

`SPEC.md` 다 채워졌으면 사람이 읽기 좋은 요약 출력:
```
## 설계 요약

### 뭘 만드나
[1줄]

### 엔드포인트
[URL + 1줄 설명, 5개 정도]

### 핵심 결정 3개
- Decision A: [선택 / 이유 / 재고 조건]
- Decision B: [선택 / 이유 / 재고 조건]
- Decision C: [선택 / 이유 / 재고 조건]

### Assumptions (6개)
[목록]

### Ambiguities I noticed (3개)
[목록]

### Out of scope
[목록 + 왜]

### JD Signal Map 매핑
[11개 signal에 매핑된 위치 표]

### 5 Design Principles + Agent Architecture
[각각의 1줄 적용]

### Anti-pattern 회피 (PROTOCOL §Anti-Patterns 11종)
[user's plan to avoid each]

### LESSONS_LEARNED 적용
[직전 실수 중 이 assignment에 적용된 것]
```

그 다음 묻기:
```
설계 리뷰해줘. 가정 중 틀린 거 있으면 지금. 없으면 "응" 하면 CODEX_PROMPTS.md 만들게.
```

수정 요청이면 `SPEC.md` 업데이트 후 재출력. "응" 받으면 Phase H.

---

## Phase H — CODEX_PROMPTS.md 생성

PROTOCOL §Phase 3의 Agent A / Agent B 프롬프트 + `_shared/agents/{builder,reviewer,tester}.md`의 sub-agent role을 사용자 assignment specifics로 customize.

### H.1 — Worktree setup commands
PROTOCOL §Phase 3 "Worktree Setup" 섹션의 bash 그대로.

### H.2 — Agent A prompt (Routes + LLM)
PROTOCOL §Phase 3의 Agent A 프롬프트 + `_shared/agents/builder.md`의 builder identity 결합. 다음만 customize:
- LLM 모델 (`claude-sonnet-4-6` 등)
- Entity 이름 (user spec에 맞춰)
- Brand 값 (efood / glovo / talabat 등)

### H.3 — Agent B prompt (Guardrails + Tests)
PROTOCOL §Phase 3의 Agent B 프롬프트 + `_shared/agents/builder.md` (impl) + `_shared/agents/tester.md` (test cases) 결합. 다음만 customize:
- 룰 셋 (R1–R5가 default)
- AGENTS.md 위치
- Sample patch (PROTOCOL의 SAMPLE_PATCH가 default)

### H.4 — Wire integration prompt
두 worktree merge 후 `src/routes.py`에서 guardrail 호출 wiring.

### H.5 — Final prompt (Phase 5 JD Alignment + Phase 6 Requirements Gate + README sync)
PROTOCOL §Phase 5의 11개 자동 체크 + §Phase 6의 requirements gate + `_shared/README_5sections.md` 템플릿 적용을 codex가 한 번에 실행:
```
After all worktrees merged:
1. Run all PROTOCOL §Phase 5 JD Alignment Check commands. Report each ✓ or ✗.
2. Run all PROTOCOL §Phase 6 Requirements Gate checks. Fill the report template verbatim.
3. Update README.md per `/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/README_5sections.md` template.
4. Cross-check: every endpoint in README §Architecture exists in @router. decorators; every claim has corresponding code.
5. Stop before commit. Show diff + 3 commit message options (Conventional Commits style).
Do NOT commit until I confirm.
```

`CODEX_PROMPTS.md`에 다음 순서로 박음:
```
### Prompt #0 — Worktree Setup (run yourself in bash)
### Prompt #1 — Agent A: Routes + LLM (paste to codex in wt-routes)
### Prompt #2 — Agent B: Guardrails + Tests (paste to codex in wt-guardrails)
### Prompt #3 — Wire integration (after both merged)
### Prompt #4 — JD Alignment + Requirements Gate + README finalize
```

각 prompt에 *"Stop before commit. Show diff + 3 commit message options. Wait for my 'commit with message X'."* 박혀 있음.

---

## End — 마지막 메시지

```
설계 끝.

📂 SPEC.md              ← 너의 결정 흔적 (평가자가 README와 cross-check)
📂 CODEX_PROMPTS.md     ← codex 실행 순서 5단계
📂 _shared/             ← 정본 (PROTOCOL, LESSONS, agent-architecture, README template, agents/*)

다음 행동:
1. SPEC.md 정독 — 본인 단어인지 확인
2. CODEX_PROMPTS.md의 Prompt #0 (worktree setup) 직접 실행
3. Prompt #1 (Agent A) → codex의 wt-routes worktree에 던지기
4. Prompt #2 (Agent B) → codex의 wt-guardrails worktree에 던지기 (#1과 동시)
5. Prompt #3 (integration) → 둘 다 merge 후 wire-up
6. Prompt #4 (JD alignment + final gate + README) → 모든 검증 + commit confirm

각 codex prompt는 "stop before commit, show diff + 3 commit messages, wait for my confirm" 박혀 있음.

세션 끝나면 잊지 말고 LESSONS_LEARNED.md에 새 mistake/learning 1~3개 append — 다음 세션이 같은 실수 안 함.

막히거나 새 결정 떠오르면 /assignment-design 다시 호출 — 새 Decision append됨.
구현 사이클 디테일은 codex-implement-workflow.md (이 폴더 부모).
```

---

## 안전장치

- `_shared/ASSIGNMENT_EXECUTION_PROTOCOL.md` 못 읽으면 stop. 경로 확인 요청.
- `_shared/LESSONS_LEARNED.md` 없으면 경고 + 비어있는 default로 진행.
- spec 못 받았으면 Phase A에서 stop.
- 사용자가 "추천대로 가자" 하면 protocol default를 그대로 박되 *"per PROTOCOL.md default, accepted by user"* 명시.
- 사용자가 PROTOCOL의 mandate 항목 (deterministic-sandwich, 3-tier schema, brand field, trace_id, tool_use)을 빼려 하면 거부 + JD signal 손실 경고: *"PROTOCOL §X에서 mandate. 빼면 JD signal Y가 사라져. 그래도 빼?"*
- "다 알아서 결정해줘" → 거부.

---

## Companion docs

| 파일 | 역할 |
|---|---|
| `codex-implement-workflow.md` (부모 폴더) | 구현 사이클 — codex와의 대화 패턴 |
| `~/.claude/commands/assignment.md` | 자동 풀 파이프라인 (대안 — 사용자 결정 안 받음) |

이 SKILL은 PROTOCOL의 "사용자 주도 설계 단계"만 분리한 conductor. `_shared/`가 바뀌면 SKILL은 자동으로 새 default 사용.

## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the t… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- assignment-design: User-driven design Q&A for take-home / case-study assignments. Loads the canonical AgenticIDE shared playbook (`/Users/mae/Desktop/Develop/AgenticIDE/_shared/`) — protocol, lessons learned, README template, agent architecture, sub-agent definitions — then walks the user through each protocol phase one decision at a time. Never decides for the user. Never writes code. Records every decision to SPEC.md in the current project directory. At the end, generates CODEX_PROMPTS.md using the agent prompts from PROTOCOL §Phase 3 customized to the user's assignment. Use when user types /assignment-design, /decide, "design before implementing", "let's design first", "코코 같이 설계하자", or starts a take-home / 과제 / mini-project. Do NOT use when debugging existing code or already mid-implementation.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- diff-review: Explain an implementation diff and produce a prioritized code review checklist. Use when the user asks for /diff-review, local code review, diff explanation, or what reviewers should inspect first.
- review: Multi-persona code review — Linus, Fowler, and Vogels each review a PR and have a discussion round. Uses local Claude token. Run as /review <PR_NUMBER>.
- fastapi: FastAPI best practices — project structure, route patterns, Pydantic v2, dependency injection, settings, error handling. References: fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, benavlabs/FastAPI-boilerplate, zhanymkanov/fastapi-best-practices, fastapi-practices/fastapi_best_architecture.
- changelog-generator: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- feature: Interactive feature workflow — gather requirements interactively (description, API list, branch name, context), then run design → implement → PR in sequence. Use when the user wants to build a new feature end-to-end.
- plan-for-codex: Analyze the user's request and write a PLAN.md at the worktree root that Codex (running in another tab on the same worktree) can read and implement. Do not modify any code yourself — only produce the plan. Use when the user wants Claude to design and Codex to implement.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- assignment: End-to-end assignment workflow — loads tech stack constraints → /think (design, 7 stages) → /iterate (Codex implements) → README polish → final gate. Usage: /assignment <assignment description or path>
- evaluate: Score a submission against the IC1 Backend 5-axis rubric (25 points). Usage: /evaluate [path-to-submission]
- diff-review: Diff Review Brief
- iterate: Multi-agent iteration loop with human checkpoints — Pre-build → Builder → Spot-check → Tester → Reviewer → Human. Usage: /iterate <feature description>
- code-review: Multi-axis code review across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use before merging any change — from yourself, another agent, or a human. Usage: /code-review [PR# | git ref | empty for current diff]
- plan: Design Agent — 3-phase collaborative design with human checkpoints at each stage. Outputs REQUIREMENTS.md + PLAN.md. Usage: /plan
- design: Analyze requirements and create a design spec before implementation. Writes to /tmp/spec.md and waits for user approval.
- think: Design thinking partner — walks alongside you through 7-stage expert planning. Shows the internal monologue of a senior engineer at each stage, then asks sharp questions to sharpen your thinking. Usage: /think <problem or decision>
- assignment: Execute the Agentic IDE take-home assignment playbook
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Phase A를 시작할게 — PROTOCOL.md와 LESSONS_LEARNED.md를 동시에 읽는다.

content.content:
1	# Lessons Learned — Agentic IDE Take-Home Sessions
2	
3	> Updated after each session. Read this before starting Phase 3 implementation.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Session: case-01-session-backend (2026-05-08)
8	
9	### Mistake 1 — Agent에게 위임 후 `load_dotenv()` 누락
10	
11	**무슨 일**: Agent A가 `src/main.py`를 작성할 때 `load_dotenv()`를 누락했다.
12	`.env`에 API 키가 있었는데도 서버가 mock 모드로 동작했고, 실제 LLM 호출이 안 된다고 착각했다.
13	
14	**왜 발생**: Agent prompt에 "dotenv를 로드하라"는 명시적 지시가 없었다.
15	merge 후 cross-cutting concern (env loading, logging config) 검토를 하지 않았다.
16	
17	**다음에 할 것**:
18	- Agent A prompt에 반드시 명시: `"from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()` must be the first thing in main.py, before any other local imports"`
19	- Merge 직후 체크리스트: `grep -r "load_dotenv" src/` → 없으면 즉시 추가
20	
21	---
22	
23	### Mistake 2 — `max_tokens=1024`로 LLM 응답 잘림
24	
25	**무슨 일**: `generate_list()` 응답이 1024 토큰을 초과해서 JSON이 중간에 잘렸다.
26	`JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string` 발생.
27	
28	**왜 발생**: "짧은 응답이면 충분하겠지"라고 가정했다.
29	실제로는 PlanStep 배열 + 설명문이 1024 토큰을 넘는 경우가 흔하다.
30	
31	**다음에 할 것**:
32	- LLM 호출 시 `max_tokens`는 항상 **4096** 이상으로 시작한다.
33	- 특히 list 반환 함수는 더 크게 잡는다 (8192도 괜찮다, 출력 토큰 과금이지 입력 아님).
34	
35	---
36	
37	### Mistake 3 — 자유 텍스트 JSON 파싱이 불안정 (tool_use 안 쓴 것)
38	
39	**무슨 일**: 시스템 프롬프트에 "JSON으로만 응답해"라고 지시했지만,
40	Claude가 응답을 ` ```json ... ``` ` 마크다운으로 감싸서 반환했다.
41	`json.loads(raw)` 실패 → `_parse_json()` 코드펜스 제거 로직 추가 → 그래도 응답 잘림으로 실패.
42	
43	**왜 발생**: 처음부터 Anthropic SDK의 `tool_use` (structured output) 를 쓰지 않았다.
44	"프롬프트로 JSON 유도"는 항상 불안정하다. Claude는 마크다운으로 감싸거나, 설명문을 붙이거나, 토큰 제한에 걸리면 JSON을 잘라버린다.
45	
46	**다음에 할 것**:
47	- **LLM에서 구조화된 출력이 필요하면 항상 `tool_use`를 쓴다.** 예외 없음.
48	- 올바른 패턴:
49	  ```python
50	  response = client.messages.create(
51	      model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
52	      max_tokens=4096,
53	      tools=[{"name": "output", "description": "...", "input_schema": schema.model_json_schema()}],
54	      tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output"},
55	      messages=[...],
56	  )
57	  result = response.content[0].input  # 이미 dict, json.loads 불필요
58	  ```
59	- list 반환이 필요하면 wrapper schema 사용:
60	  ```python
61	  list_schema = {
62	      "type": "object",
63	      "properties": {"items": {"type": "array", "items": schema.model_json_schema()}},
64	      "required": ["items"],
65	  }
66	  # → result["items"] 로 꺼냄
67	  ```
68	
69	---
70	
71	### Mistake 4 — E2E 검증 스크립트에서 false negative
72	
73	**무슨 일**: `curl | python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['id'])"` 가
74	JSON 문자열 안에 `\n`이 있을 때 `Invalid control character` 에러를 냈다.
75	서버는 정상인데 검증 스크립트가 실패해서 "서버가 broken"으로 오판했다.
76	
77	**왜 발생**: bash 파이프로 JSON을 python3 인라인 코드에 넘기면 제어문자 처리가 취약하다.
78	특히 diff나 코드가 포함된 응답에서 자주 발생한다.
79	
80	**다음에 할 것**:
81	- E2E 검증 시 항상 **파일로 받아서 파싱**하거나 `python3 -m json.tool`로 출력 확인만 한다.
82	  ```bash
83	  curl -s ... -o /tmp/response.json
84	  python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/response.json')); print(d['id'])"
85	  ```
86	- 또는 `jq`가 있으면: `curl -s ... | jq '.[0].id'`
87	- 서버 로그에서 HTTP 200이 나오면 서버는 정상. 스크립트 파싱 문제와 구분한다.
88	
89	---
90	
91	### Mistake 5 — ruff E402 (import 순서)
92	
93	**무슨 일**: `load_dotenv()`를 import 사이에 넣어서 ruff가 E402를 냈다.
94	```python
95	from dotenv import load_dotenv
96	load_dotenv()            # ← 여기
97	from fastapi import FastAPI  # E402: module level import not at top of file
98	```
99	
100	**다음에 할 것**:
101	- `load_dotenv()`는 `# noqa: E402` 처리하거나, ruff 설정에서 예외 추가.
102	- `pyproject.toml`에 미리 추가:
103	  ```toml
104	  [tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
105	  "src/main.py" = ["E402"]
106	  ```
107	
108	---
109	
110	---
111	
112	### Mistake 6 — LLM에 full schema 넘겨서 nested children까지 채워짐
113	
114	**무슨 일**: `generate_list(prompt, brand, PlanStep)` 호출 시 `PlanStep.model_json_schema()`를
115	tool_use input_schema로 그대로 넘겼다. `PlanStep` 스키마에 `patches: list[PatchProposal]`가 포함되어 있어서
116	LLM이 "친절하게" patches와 checks까지 전부 채워 반환했다.
117	결과: POST /plan이 plan + patch + check를 한 번에 다 하는 것처럼 동작.
118	
119	**왜 발생**: 도메인 모델 schema(저장/응답용)와 LLM input schema(생성 요청용)를 분리하지 않았다.
120	
121	**다음에 할 것**:
122	- LLM 호출용 input schema를 별도로 정의한다: `PlanStepInput`, `PatchProposalInput`
123	- 규칙: **LLM input schema는 LLM이 생성해야 할 필드만 포함. id, created_at, 자식 배열 제외.**
124	- `generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput)` → `PlanStep(**result)` 로 변환
125	- POST /plan 응답에서 `patches: []` 인지 항상 확인한다.
126	
127	---
128	
129	---
130	
131	### Mistake 7 — guardrails가 AGENTS.md를 실제로 읽지 않음 (case-01-session-second)
132	
133	**무슨 일**: `guardrails.run_checks(diff)`가 브랜드를 받지 않았고, severity를 코드에 하드코딩했다.
134	`llm._plan_prompt()`도 AGENTS.md 내용을 LLM에 전달하지 않았다.
135	`efood/AGENTS.md`를 수정해도 guardrail 동작이 바뀌지 않는 상태였다.
136	
137	**왜 발생**: Protocol의 Agent B 프롬프트에 "brand parameter is present for future use, **even if unused now**"라고 적혀 있었다.
138	에이전트가 이 표현을 "브랜드를 받되 쓰지 않아도 된다"로 해석했다.
139	Phase 5 JD Alignment Check가 체크박스 테이블이라 자동 검증이 없었고, 90분 제약 안에서 눈으로 확인하지 못했다.
140	
141	**핵심 원인**: "brand parameter accepted but unused" = 구현 누락. 파라미터 시그니처만 있고 파일을 읽지 않으면 요구사항 미충족.
142	
143	**다음에 할 것**:
144	- Protocol Agent B 프롬프트에서 "unused" 표현 제거 → `_parse_severities(brand)` 구현 명시
145	- Phase 5 JD Alignment Check를 체크박스 → 실행 가능한 bash 검증 스크립트로 교체
146	- 리트머스 테스트: `efood/AGENTS.md`의 R1 severity를 WARN→BLOCK으로 바꾸고 테스트 재실행 → 결과가 바뀌어야 함
147	- Anti-pattern #7/#11 추가: "guardrail이 AGENTS.md를 읽지 않으면 구현 누락"
148	
149	---
150	
151	## Agent Prompt 필수 체크리스트 (Phase 3 시작 전)
152	
153	Agent A (routes + LLM) prompt에 반드시 포함할 것:
154	
155	```
156	- from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv() must be FIRST in main.py, before any local imports
157	- All LLM calls: use tool_use with tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}, max_tokens=4096
158	- Never use json.loads() on raw LLM response text — use response.content[0].input directly
159	- No print() anywhere — use logging
160	```
161	
162	Agent B (guardrails) prompt에 반드시 포함할 것:
163	
164	```
165	- No LLM calls in this file — deterministic regex only
166	- Return one GuardrailCheck per rule always (pass or fail)
167	- brand parameter must be in the signature even if unused
168	```
169	
170	---
171	
172	## E2E 검증 안전한 패턴
173	
174	```bash
175	# 응답을 파일로 받아서 파싱
176	curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/sessions \
177	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
178	  -d '{"title":"test","description":"test","brand":"efood"}' \
179	  -o /tmp/session.json
180	
181	SESSION=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('/tmp/session.json'))['id'])")
182	```
183

# Lessons Learned — Agentic IDE Take-Home Sessions

> Updated after each session. Read this before starting Phase 3 implementation.

---

## Session: case-01-session-backend (2026-05-08)

### Mistake 1 — Agent에게 위임 후 `load_dotenv()` 누락

**무슨 일**: Agent A가 `src/main.py`를 작성할 때 `load_dotenv()`를 누락했다.
`.env`에 API 키가 있었는데도 서버가 mock 모드로 동작했고, 실제 LLM 호출이 안 된다고 착각했다.

**왜 발생**: Agent prompt에 "dotenv를 로드하라"는 명시적 지시가 없었다.
merge 후 cross-cutting concern (env loading, logging config) 검토를 하지 않았다.

**다음에 할 것**:
- Agent A prompt에 반드시 명시: `"from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()` must be the first thing in main.py, before any other local imports"`
- Merge 직후 체크리스트: `grep -r "load_dotenv" src/` → 없으면 즉시 추가

---

### Mistake 2 — `max_tokens=1024`로 LLM 응답 잘림

**무슨 일**: `generate_list()` 응답이 1024 토큰을 초과해서 JSON이 중간에 잘렸다.
`JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string` 발생.

**왜 발생**: "짧은 응답이면 충분하겠지"라고 가정했다.
실제로는 PlanStep 배열 + 설명문이 1024 토큰을 넘는 경우가 흔하다.

**다음에 할 것**:
- LLM 호출 시 `max_tokens`는 항상 **4096** 이상으로 시작한다.
- 특히 list 반환 함수는 더 크게 잡는다 (8192도 괜찮다, 출력 토큰 과금이지 입력 아님).

---

### Mistake 3 — 자유 텍스트 JSON 파싱이 불안정 (tool_use 안 쓴 것)

**무슨 일**: 시스템 프롬프트에 "JSON으로만 응답해"라고 지시했지만,
Claude가 응답을 ` ```json ... ``` ` 마크다운으로 감싸서 반환했다.
`json.loads(raw)` 실패 → `_parse_json()` 코드펜스 제거 로직 추가 → 그래도 응답 잘림으로 실패.

**왜 발생**: 처음부터 Anthropic SDK의 `tool_use` (structured output) 를 쓰지 않았다.
"프롬프트로 JSON 유도"는 항상 불안정하다. Claude는 마크다운으로 감싸거나, 설명문을 붙이거나, 토큰 제한에 걸리면 JSON을 잘라버린다.

**다음에 할 것**:
- **LLM에서 구조화된 출력이 필요하면 항상 `tool_use`를 쓴다.** 예외 없음.
- 올바른 패턴:
  ```python
  response = client.messages.create(
      model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
      max_tokens=4096,
      tools=[{"name": "output", "description": "...", "input_schema": schema.model_json_schema()}],
      tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "output"},
      messages=[...],
  )
  result = response.content[0].input  # 이미 dict, json.loads 불필요
  ```
- list 반환이 필요하면 wrapper schema 사용:
  ```python
  list_schema = {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {"items": {"type": "array", "items": schema.model_json_schema()}},
      "required": ["items"],
  }
  # → result["items"] 로 꺼냄
  ```

---

### Mistake 4 — E2E 검증 스크립트에서 false negative

**무슨 일**: `curl | python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['id'])"` 가
JSON 문자열 안에 `\n`이 있을 때 `Invalid control character` 에러를 냈다.
서버는 정상인데 검증 스크립트가 실패해서 "서버가 broken"으로 오판했다.

**왜 발생**: bash 파이프로 JSON을 python3 인라인 코드에 넘기면 제어문자 처리가 취약하다.
특히 diff나 코드가 포함된 응답에서 자주 발생한다.

**다음에 할 것**:
- E2E 검증 시 항상 **파일로 받아서 파싱**하거나 `python3 -m json.tool`로 출력 확인만 한다.
  ```bash
  curl -s ... -o /tmp/response.json
  python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/response.json')); print(d['id'])"
  ```
- 또는 `jq`가 있으면: `curl -s ... | jq '.[0].id'`
- 서버 로그에서 HTTP 200이 나오면 서버는 정상. 스크립트 파싱 문제와 구분한다.

---

### Mistake 5 — ruff E402 (import 순서)

**무슨 일**: `load_dotenv()`를 import 사이에 넣어서 ruff가 E402를 냈다.
```python
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()            # ← 여기
from fastapi import FastAPI  # E402: module level import not at top of file
```

**다음에 할 것**:
- `load_dotenv()`는 `# noqa: E402` 처리하거나, ruff 설정에서 예외 추가.
- `pyproject.toml`에 미리 추가:
  ```toml
  [tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
  "src/main.py" = ["E402"]
  ```

---

---

### Mistake 6 — LLM에 full schema 넘겨서 nested children까지 채워짐

**무슨 일**: `generate_list(prompt, brand, PlanStep)` 호출 시 `PlanStep.model_json_schema()`를
tool_use input_schema로 그대로 넘겼다. `PlanStep` 스키마에 `patches: list[PatchProposal]`가 포함되어 있어서
LLM이 "친절하게" patches와 checks까지 전부 채워 반환했다.
결과: POST /plan이 plan + patch + check를 한 번에 다 하는 것처럼 동작.

**왜 발생**: 도메인 모델 schema(저장/응답용)와 LLM input schema(생성 요청용)를 분리하지 않았다.

**다음에 할 것**:
- LLM 호출용 input schema를 별도로 정의한다: `PlanStepInput`, `PatchProposalInput`
- 규칙: **LLM input schema는 LLM이 생성해야 할 필드만 포함. id, created_at, 자식 배열 제외.**
- `generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput)` → `PlanStep(**result)` 로 변환
- POST /plan 응답에서 `patches: []` 인지 항상 확인한다.

---

---

### Mistake 7 — guardrails가 AGENTS.md를 실제로 읽지 않음 (case-01-session-second)

**무슨 일**: `guardrails.run_checks(diff)`가 브랜드를 받지 않았고, severity를 코드에 하드코딩했다.
`llm._plan_prompt()`도 AGENTS.md 내용을 LLM에 전달하지 않았다.
`efood/AGENTS.md`를 수정해도 guardrail 동작이 바뀌지 않는 상태였다.

**왜 발생**: Protocol의 Agent B 프롬프트에 "brand parameter is present for future use, **even if unused now**"라고 적혀 있었다.
에이전트가 이 표현을 "브랜드를 받되 쓰지 않아도 된다"로 해석했다.
Phase 5 JD Alignment Check가 체크박스 테이블이라 자동 검증이 없었고, 90분 제약 안에서 눈으로 확인하지 못했다.

**핵심 원인**: "brand parameter accepted but unused" = 구현 누락. 파라미터 시그니처만 있고 파일을 읽지 않으면 요구사항 미충족.

**다음에 할 것**:
- Protocol Agent B 프롬프트에서 "unused" 표현 제거 → `_parse_severities(brand)` 구현 명시
- Phase 5 JD Alignment Check를 체크박스 → 실행 가능한 bash 검증 스크립트로 교체
- 리트머스 테스트: `efood/AGENTS.md`의 R1 severity를 WARN→BLOCK으로 바꾸고 테스트 재실행 → 결과가 바뀌어야 함
- Anti-pattern #7/#11 추가: "guardrail이 AGENTS.md를 읽지 않으면 구현 누락"

## Agent Prompt 필수 체크리스트 (Phase 3 시작 전)

Agent A (routes + LLM) prompt에 반드시 포함할 것:

```
- from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv() must be FIRST in main.py, before any local imports
- All LLM calls: use tool_use with tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}, max_tokens=4096
- Never use json.loads() on raw LLM response text — use response.content[0].input directly
- No print() anywhere — use logging
```

Agent B (guardrails) prompt에 반드시 포함할 것:

```
- No LLM calls in this file — deterministic regex only
- Return one GuardrailCheck per rule always (pass or fail)
- brand parameter must be in the signature even if unused
```

  -d '{"title":"test","description":"test","brand":"efood"}' \
  -o /tmp/session.json

SESSION=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('/tmp/session.json'))['id'])")
```

10	## JD Signal Map — Read Before Any Code
11	
12	Every implementation choice below maps to a JD keyword. If a choice is not on this map, it is P2.
13	
14	| JD Keyword | What to show in code | Where it lands |
15	|---|---|---|
16	| `Git worktrees` | Two worktrees, parallel branches, explicit merge step | Phase 3 setup, commit history |
17	| `multi-agent` | Agent A (routes) + Agent B (guardrails) on separate worktrees | Phase 3 prompt framing |
18	| `AGENTS.md / Engineering Manifesto` | File loaded just-in-time in every LLM call; brand-specific override path described | `src/llm.py`, README §Architecture |
19	| `guardrails — safe to deploy` | `GuardrailCheck(ruleId, severity, result, reason)` — BLOCK stops merge | `src/guardrails.py`, README §Domain Model |
20	| `multi-brand (efood/glovo/talabat)` | `brand` field on Session; guardrail loader parametrized by brand | Schema, guardrail signature |
21	| `context integration` | AGENTS.md injected as system-prompt context, not hardcoded strings | `src/llm.py` system prompt |
22	| `OTEL / OAM / tracing` | `trace_id` on Session; P2 note for OAM export | Schema + README §If More Time |
23	| `deterministic boundary` | Regex-first guardrails; LLM only for plan/patch generation | README §Architecture diagram |
24	| `developer productivity / friction` | README §Problem opens with the manual workflow this replaces | README §1 Problem |
25	| `customizing cutting-edge agentic IDE` | "This is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer above Cursor" | README §1 first line |
26	| `measurement / KPI` | `GuardrailCheck` output feeds PR/engineer and Human/AI code ratio dashboards | README §If More Time |
27	
28	**Mandatory before submitting**: every row above must map to ≥1 line of code or README text.
29	
30	---
31	
32	## Design Principles — Apply Before Writing Any Schema or Route
33	
34	These five principles gate every implementation decision. If a choice conflicts with one, stop and reconsider.
35	
36	### 1. SRP — Single Responsibility per Endpoint
37	
38	Each endpoint = one state transition = one responsibility.
39	
40	| Endpoint | Responsibility | What it must NOT do |
41	|---|---|---|
42	| `POST /sessions` | Create session entity | Generate plan, touch LLM |
43	| `POST /sessions/{id}/plan` | Decompose description → `[PlanStep]` with `patches: []` | Generate diffs, run guardrails |
44	| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches` | Generate diff for one PlanStep → `PatchProposal` with `checks: []` | Run guardrails, modify other steps |
45	| `POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check` | Run guardrails → `[GuardrailCheck]` | Generate new diffs, re-plan |
46	| `GET /sessions/{id}` | Return accumulated state | Trigger any computation |
47	
48	**Smell check**: if a route calls both `generate()` and `check_patch()`, it violates SRP.
49	
50	**Schema SRP corollary**: domain models (storage/response) and LLM input schemas are different things.
51	- Domain model: `PlanStep(id, description, target_files, patches=[])` — full state with children
52	- LLM input schema: `PlanStepInput(description, target_files)` — only what the LLM generates
53	- Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM if it contains nested children (id, created_at, child lists).
54	  The LLM will fill them in, breaking the SRP of every downstream endpoint.
55	
56	---
57	
58	### 2. Deterministic Boundary
59	
60	The LLM is always sandwiched between deterministic code. Non-determinism is quarantined.
61	
62	```
63	[deterministic in]  →  [LLM]  →  [deterministic out]
64	  schema validation     proposes    schema validation
65	  AGENTS.md load        only        guardrail regex
66	  brand resolution               Pydantic parsing
67	```
68	
69	Rules:
70	- `src/guardrails.py` contains **zero** LLM calls. Regex only.
71	- `src/llm.py` contains **zero** business logic. It is a transport layer.
72	- LLM output is always parsed through Pydantic before it touches application state.
73	- Use `tool_use` + `tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}` — never free-text JSON parsing.
74	
75	---
76	
77	### 3. YAGNI — You Aren't Gonna Need It
78	
79	Build exactly what the spec asks. Nothing more.
80	
81	| Temptation | Decision |
82	|---|---|
83	| SQLite / Postgres | In-memory dict. Persistence is P2 — document the swap path. |
84	| Auth / API keys | Not in spec. Padding scope is a red flag for evaluators. |
85	| LLM-as-judge for guardrails | Regex covers R1–R5 exactly. LLM-as-judge is P2. |
86	| Retry loop / Evaluator-Optimizer | P2. Document it in README §If More Time. |
87	| Multi-brand AGENTS.md routing | `brand` parameter in signature. Second brand file is P2. |
88	| Caching | Not needed within a 90-min session lifetime. |
89	
90	If it is not in the spec and not a JD signal, it is P2 — mention in README, do not implement.
91	
92	**YAGNI applies to response shape too — Response Schema = Workflow Signal.**
93	
94	The shape of a response communicates what the endpoint did. Empty child collections in a response are not "free" — they send a false signal.
95	
96	```
97	# Wrong: POST /plan returns this
98	{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...], "patches": []}
99	#                                                           ^^^^^^^^^^
100	#                                              implies patches are part of planning
101	
102	# Right: POST /plan returns this
103	{"id": "...", "description": "...", "target_files": [...]}
104	# shape says: "I produced a plan step. patches are someone else's job."
105	```
106	
107	Rule: **each endpoint's response schema contains only the fields that endpoint created.**
108	Use a dedicated response model (e.g. `PlanStepOut`, `PatchProposalOut`) without child lists — do not reuse the full domain model as the response type.
109	
110	```python
111	# models.py — response schemas (one per endpoint that creates a new entity)
112	class PlanStepOut(BaseModel):
113	    id: UUID
114	    description: str
115	    target_files: list[str]
116	    # no patches — POST /plan does not create patches
117	
118	class PatchProposalOut(BaseModel):
119	    id: UUID
120	    planStepId: UUID
121	    diff: str
122	    # no checks — POST /patches does not run guardrails
123	```
124	
125	The full domain model (`PlanStep` with `patches`, `PatchProposal` with `checks`) is only used in `GET /sessions/{id}` — the accumulated state view.
126	
127	---
128	
129	### 4. Workflow-first Design
130	
131	Design the workflow (sequence of state transitions) before designing schemas or routes.
132	
133	**Step 1 — Draw the workflow as a state machine first:**
134	```
135	∅ → Session(steps=[]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[])]) → Session(steps=[PlanStep(patches=[PatchProposal(checks=[])])]) → Session(steps=[...(checks=[GuardrailCheck...])])
136	```
137	
138	**Step 2 — Each arrow = one endpoint.**
139	Each endpoint advances state by exactly one step. No endpoint skips a step or does two steps at once.
140	
141	**Step 3 — Schema flows from workflow, not from database convenience.**
142	Ask: "what is the minimal input the LLM needs to do its job?" → that is the LLM input schema.
143	Ask: "what is the full state a reviewing engineer needs?" → that is the domain model.
144	
145	---
146	
147	### 5. Explainability
148	
149	Every AI decision must be traceable by a human reviewer.
150	
151	| Layer | Explainability mechanism |
152	|---|---|
153	| Plan generation | `PlanStep.description` is human-readable; `target_files` is explicit |
154	| Patch generation | `PatchProposal.diff` is a standard unified diff — reviewable in any diff tool |
155	| Guardrail result | `GuardrailCheck.ruleId` + `reason` — cites the specific AGENTS.md rule |
156	| Session audit | `Session.trace_id` — future OTEL/OAM hook |
157	| AI vs human work | README §AI Leverage table — explicit, honest accounting |
158	
159	**Rule**: No GuardrailCheck result without a `reason` that names the specific rule (`per AGENTS.md R4`).
160	A `result: "fail"` with `reason: "error"` is worse than useless — it is unactionable.
161	
162	---
163	
164	## Meta-Principles (from Anthropic engineering blog + agent-architecture.md)
165	
166	- **Deterministic sandwich**: Every LLM call sits between deterministic input validation and deterministic output verification. The LLM proposes; the code decides.
167	- **Workflow backbone + one agent loop**: LLM loop only where genuinely non-deterministic. Guardrails are always deterministic.
168	- **Just-in-time context**: Load AGENTS.md at call time — never pre-dump into module-level startup.
169	- **Single agent > multi-agent for the coding loop**: Use parallel worktrees for *independent implementation tracks*, not for agents collaborating on the same task.
170	- **README is the deliverable**: Code is proof it works. The README demonstrates you think like a system designer.
171	- **DH framing**: This service is a "DH-aware integration layer" — it augments Cursor/Claude Code with brand context and guardrails. It is not a replacement IDE.
172	
173	## Slash Command Contract — `/assignment`
174	
175	The project command lives in both `.claude/commands/assignment.md` and
176	`case-01-session-backend/.claude/commands/assignment.md`. Keep them in sync.
177	
178	Pattern borrowed from Anthropic's `anthropics/claude-code` project commands:
179	
180	- Use command frontmatter (`description`, `allowed-tools`) so the command advertises its purpose and narrows tool use.
181	- Inject live context at invocation time (`git status`, assignment text, protocol, AGENTS.md) instead of relying on stale memory.
182	- Treat `$ARGUMENTS` as extra user input, not as a replacement for the assignment or protocol.
183	- Start with a todo list and update it as phases complete.
184	- Never invent requirements. If the assignment is ambiguous, document the assumption rather than expanding scope.
185	- Be conservative with automation: do not push, open PRs, or post external comments unless the user explicitly asks.
186	- End every `/assignment` run with files changed, verification run, and remaining checks.
187	
188	If a command behavior conflicts with this protocol, the protocol wins.

225	## Phase 1 — README Skeleton + Domain Model (0:05 – 0:20)
226	
227	### 1.1 README.md — Write the 5 sections in English, all blanks filled with placeholders
228	
229	```markdown
230	# Session Backend — Agentic IDE Integration Layer
231	
232	> This service is the DH-aware integration layer that sits above Cursor/Claude Code:
233	> it injects brand context (AGENTS.md), proposes code patches via LLM, and enforces
234	> guardrails before any generated diff can be merged.
235	> The LLM proposes; deterministic checks decide.
236	
237	## 1. Problem & Approach
238	
239	**What this replaces**: a developer manually reviewing LLM-generated diffs against
240	their brand's Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) before merging.
241	
242	**Architecture**: deterministic sandwich —
243	- deterministic input (AGENTS.md loaded just-in-time, brand resolved from session)
244	- LLM call (plan decomposition or patch generation, schema-constrained output)
245	- deterministic output (guardrail regex checks, severity-gated merge decision)
246	
247	**Assumptions** (5+):
248	1. In-memory storage is sufficient for a session-scoped prototype; persistence is P2.
249	2. AGENTS.md lives at the repo root and is the authoritative rule source.
250	3. Severity BLOCK = merge blocked; WARN = visible to developer but not blocking.
251	4. LLM output is always validated against the Pydantic schema before storage.
252	5. Each brand (efood/glovo/talabat) can have its own AGENTS.md path; currently efood only.
253	6. `trace_id` is generated per-session for future OTEL export.
254	
255	**Ambiguities I noticed**:
256	1. Should WARN rules accumulate across patches and block after N warnings?
257	2. Is the LLM allowed to propose changes to AGENTS.md itself?
258	3. Who owns rule addition — platform team or brand team?
259	
260	## 2. Domain Model
261	
262	- `Session` — top-level entity; carries `brand`, `trace_id`, and all child lists
263	- `PlanStep` — one proposed change (description + target_files)
264	- `PatchProposal` — unified diff string for one PlanStep
265	- `GuardrailCheck` — one rule result: `ruleId`, `severity`, `result`, `reason`
266	
267	```
268	Session 1—* PlanStep 1—* PatchProposal 1—* GuardrailCheck
269	```
270	
271	Severity ladder: `BLOCK` (security/brand violation) > `WARN` (style) > `INFO` (suggestion).
272	A patch with any `BLOCK` check cannot be merged. This feeds the Human/AI code ratio KPI
273	by flagging which AI-generated diffs required human intervention.
274	
275	## 3. AI Leverage
276	
277	| Part | Done by | Verification |
278	|------|---------|--------------|
279	| Domain models | Hand | Type-checked by Pydantic + mypy |
280	| FastAPI routes | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/routes) | Hand-reviewed diff |
281	| Guardrail regex rules | AI (Claude Code, worktree feat/guardrails) | Pytest: R2/R4/R5 BLOCK cases |
282	| LLM prompt design | Hand | Manual smoke test with real API key |
283	| README | Hand | — |
284	
285	Every AI-generated file went through: `ruff check` → `pytest` → manual diff scan.
286	No AI output was committed without a test covering the specific behavior.
287	
288	"The LLM only performs extraction and proposal generation.
289	Merging, validation, and guardrail evaluation are deterministic."
290	
291	## 4. Trade-offs & Decisions
292	
293	| Decision | Rationale | Reconsider if |
294	|----------|-----------|---------------|
295	| In-memory storage | Removes DB setup from the critical path; session lifetime = single workflow | Sessions must survive server restart |
296	| Regex-first guardrails (not LLM-as-judge) | Deterministic, fast, testable; regex covers R1–R5 exactly | Rules become too nuanced for regex (e.g. "detect hardcoded secret") |
297	| Single-brand (efood) | Spec asks for one brand; multi-brand is a path, not a feature | Second brand onboards |
298	| No auth | Spec does not specify auth; adding it would pad scope, not signal | Service goes to staging |
299	| Direct Anthropic SDK (no LangChain) | Framework would obscure the prompt/response contract, making guardrails harder to test | Team standardizes on a shared LLM gateway |
300	| `trace_id` on every entity | Free — uuid4 at creation time; enables OTEL export without schema change | OAM integration is scoped |
301	
302	## 5. If More Time
303	
304	- **OTEL export** → push `trace_id` spans to DH OAM (Observability & Monitoring); ties into PR/engineer KPI dashboard
305	- **Multi-brand AGENTS.md override** → parametrize loader by `brand`; each brand maintains its own manifesto
306	- **SKILL.md Skills pattern** (Anthropic open standard) → per-brand capability packages loadable just-in-time
307	- **SQLite persistence** → swap in-memory dict for SQLite; no schema change required (Pydantic models already serialize cleanly)
308	- **LLM-as-judge for style rules** → R3 (docstrings) is hard to catch with regex; second-pass LLM eval for WARN-tier rules
309	- **Evaluator-Optimizer loop** → let LLM re-propose patch after BLOCK guardrail fails, up to N retries
310

316	uv run pytest            # all tests green

336

340	```
341	
342	### 1.2 Pydantic Schemas — data shape only, zero logic
343	
344	Three kinds of models. Do not confuse them.
345	
346	| Kind | Purpose | Rule |
347	|---|---|---|
348	| **LLM input schema** | What we ask the LLM to generate | Only fields the LLM creates. No id, no timestamps, no child lists. |
349	| **Response schema (Out)** | What each endpoint returns to the caller | Only fields that endpoint created. No child lists belonging to later endpoints. |
350	| **Domain model** | Storage + `GET /sessions/{id}` full state | Nested children: steps → patches → checks. Never use as LLM input or single-step response. |
351	
352	**Never pass a domain model schema to the LLM.** Child lists (`patches`, `checks`) will be filled in by the LLM, silently collapsing separate endpoints into one — violating SRP and Workflow-first.
353	
354	**Never return a domain model directly from a mutating endpoint.** Returning `PlanStep` (which includes `patches: []`) from `POST /plan` falsely implies the plan step owns patching — a structural lie in the API contract.
355	
356	Files: `src/models.py` + `src/schemas.py`
357	
358	```python
359	# ── src/models.py — 도메인 모델만 ────────────────────────────────────────────
360	from uuid import UUID
361	from datetime import datetime
362	from typing import Literal
363	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
364	
365	Brand = Literal["efood", "glovo", "talabat"]
366	Severity = Literal["BLOCK", "WARN", "INFO"]
367	CheckResult = Literal["pass", "fail"]
368	
369	class GuardrailCheck(BaseModel):
370	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
371	    ruleId: str
372	    severity: Severity
373	    result: CheckResult
374	    reason: str                     # must cite specific rule: "per AGENTS.md R4"
375	
376	class PatchProposal(BaseModel):
377	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
378	    id: UUID
379	    step_id: UUID
380	    brand: Brand
381	    diff: str
382	    checks: list[GuardrailCheck] = Field(default_factory=list)
383	    created_at: datetime
384	
385	class PlanStep(BaseModel):
386	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
387	    id: UUID
388	    description: str
389	    target_files: list[str]
390	    patches: list[PatchProposal] = Field(default_factory=list)
391	
392	class Session(BaseModel):
393	    model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)
394	    id: UUID
395	    title: str
396	    description: str
397	    brand: Brand
398	    trace_id: UUID                  # OTEL hook
399	    owner_id: str = ""
400	    steps: list[PlanStep] = Field(default_factory=list)
401	    created_at: datetime
402	
403	
404	# ── src/schemas.py — DTOs (요청/LLM/응답) ────────────────────────────────────
405	from src.models import Brand
406	
407	class SessionCreate(BaseModel):    # request DTO
408	    title: str
409	    description: str
410	    brand: Brand
411	
412	class PatchCreate(BaseModel):      # request DTO
413	    step_id: UUID
414	
415	class PlanStepInput(BaseModel):    # LLM input DTO
416	    description: str
417	    target_files: list[str]
418

421

423	    id: UUID

428	    id: UUID
429	    step_id: UUID
430	    diff: str

451	```
452	Architecture — Deterministic Sandwich:
453	
454	Developer Request
455	        │
456	        ▼
457	[POST /sessions]
458	  brand: "efood" → resolves AGENTS.md path         ← deterministic
459	
460	[POST /sessions/{id}/plan]
461	  load AGENTS.md just-in-time                       ← deterministic
462	  → LLM(claude-sonnet-4-6): decompose → [PlanStep] ← non-deterministic, schema-constrained
463	  → validate: target_files non-empty               ← deterministic
464	
465	[POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches]
466	  → LLM: generate unified diff for PlanStep        ← non-deterministic
467	  → store as PatchProposal(diff=...)
468	
469	[POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check]
470	  → load AGENTS.md for brand just-in-time          ← deterministic
471	  → for each rule: regex check → GuardrailCheck    ← deterministic
472	  → any BLOCK? → merge gated                       ← deterministic
473	
474	[GET /sessions/{id}]
475	  → full state: steps + patches + checks + trace_id
476	```

502	### Agent A Prompt — Routes + LLM Integration (worktree: wt-routes)
503	
504	```
505	You are implementing the FastAPI routes for a DH Agentic IDE session backend.
506	Branch: feat/routes-llm-integration. Worktree: ../wt-routes.
507	src/models.py is already committed on main — do NOT modify it.
508	
509	Positioning context (important for README alignment):
510	This service is the "DH-aware integration layer" above Cursor/Claude Code.
511	It injects AGENTS.md (the brand's Engineering Manifesto) as just-in-time context
512	into every LLM call — not as a pre-loaded module variable.
513	
514	Files to create:
515	  src/llm.py    — LLM client wrapper
516	  src/routes.py — 5 FastAPI route handlers
517	  src/main.py   — app factory + router registration + /health
518	
519	1. src/llm.py
520	   - Two functions: generate(prompt, brand, schema) -> dict  and  generate_list(prompt, brand, schema) -> list[dict]
521	   - Loads AGENTS.md for the brand just-in-time: Path(f"{brand}/AGENTS.md").read_text()
522	   - Uses tool_use for structured output — NEVER free-text JSON parsing:
523	       tools=[{"name":"output","description":"...","input_schema": schema.model_json_schema()}]
524	       tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output"}
525	       result = response.content[0].input   # already a dict, no json.loads needed
526	   - max_tokens=4096 minimum
527	   - generate_list wraps schema in: {"type":"object","properties":{"items":{"type":"array","items":schema.model_json_schema()}},"required":["items"]}
528	     and returns result["items"]
529	   - If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set: return deterministic mock (do not crash)
530	   - CRITICAL: routes call generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput) and generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput)
531	     — NEVER generate(prompt, brand, PlanStep) or generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposal)
532	     — Domain models contain child lists; passing them to LLM violates SRP
533	   - Functions ≤ 30 lines. No print(). Use logging.
534	
535	2. src/routes.py — all routes use sessions: dict[str, Session] from src/store.py
536	
537	   Use three-tier schemas — NEVER return a domain model from a mutating endpoint:
538	   - LLM calls: generate(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput), generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput)
539	   - POST /plan response_model=list[PlanStepOut] — return PlanStepOut per step (no patches field)
540	   - POST /patches response_model=PatchProposalOut — return PatchProposalOut (no checks field)
541	   - GET /sessions/{id} response_model=Session — only endpoint that returns full nested state
542	
543	   POST /sessions
544	     body: {title, description, brand}
545	     → Session(title=..., description=..., brand=...)
546	     → store in dict, return session
547	
548	   POST /sessions/{id}/plan
549	     → call generate_list(prompt, brand, PlanStepInput) → list[dict]
550	     → steps = [PlanStep(**s) for s in raw_steps]; attach to session.steps
551	     → return [PlanStepOut(id=s.id, description=s.description, target_files=s.target_files) for s in steps]
552	
553	   POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches
554	     step_id is a path param — no request body needed
555	     → call generate(prompt, brand, PatchProposalInput) → dict
556	     → patch = PatchProposal(**raw, planStepId=step.id); attach to step.patches
557	     → return PatchProposalOut(id=patch.id, planStepId=patch.planStepId, diff=patch.diff, created_at=patch.created_at)
558	
559	   POST /sessions/{id}/steps/{step_id}/patches/{patch_id}/check
560	     → import check_patch from src.guardrails (stub: raise ImportError gracefully if missing)
561	     → attach results to patch.checks, return list[GuardrailCheck]
562	
563	   GET /sessions/{id}
564	     → return full Session (all nested lists included)
565	
566	3. src/store.py
567	   sessions: dict[str, Session] = {}
568	
569	Constraints:
570	- All imports absolute (from src.models import ...)
571	- ruff check must pass
572	- No print() anywhere
573	
574	Done when: uvicorn src.main:app starts and GET /health returns {"status": "ok"}.

579	### Agent B Prompt — Guardrails + Tests (worktree: wt-guardrails)
580	
581	```
582	You are implementing the deterministic guardrail layer for a DH Agentic IDE session backend.
583	Branch: feat/guardrails-deterministic. Worktree: ../wt-guardrails.
584	src/models.py is already committed on main — do NOT modify it.
585	
586	JD context:
587	The guardrail is what makes AI-generated code "safe to deploy."
588	It implements the Engineering Manifesto (AGENTS.md) as code-enforced policy.
589	Every rule must produce a GuardrailCheck with ruleId, severity, result, and reason.
590	
591	File: src/guardrails.py
592	
593	  def run_checks(diff: str, brand: Brand) -> list[GuardrailCheck]:
594	      """Evaluate a unified diff against the brand's AGENTS.md rules.
595	      Returns one GuardrailCheck per rule, regardless of pass/fail.
596	      """
597	
598	MANDATORY — AGENTS.md must be the source of truth for severities:
599	
600	  def _parse_severities(brand: Brand) -> dict[str, str]:
601	      """Read {brand}/AGENTS.md and parse rule severities from it.
602	      Fall back to hardcoded defaults only if the file is missing.
603	      """
604	      try:
605	          text = Path(f"{brand}/AGENTS.md").read_text()
606	      except FileNotFoundError:
607	          return {"R1": "WARN", "R2": "BLOCK", "R3": "WARN", "R4": "BLOCK", "R5": "BLOCK"}
608	      pattern = re.compile(r"-\s+(R\d+)\s+(WARN|BLOCK|INFO):")
609	      return {m.group(1): m.group(2) for m in pattern.finditer(text)}
610	
611	  # run_checks calls _parse_severities(brand) FIRST, then passes sev.get("R1", ...) etc.
612	  # NEVER hardcode "WARN" or "BLOCK" as literals in the rule dispatch list itself.
613	
614	Rules (regex-based, deterministic — no LLM calls in this file):
615	
616	  R1 — Absolute imports only
617	    Pattern: line in diff starting with +, containing "from ." (relative import)
618	    Reason template: "Relative import detected: '{match}' — use absolute imports per AGENTS.md R1"
619	
620	  R2 — No os.system or subprocess without review
621	    Pattern: + line containing "os.system(" or "subprocess."
622	    Reason template: "Unsafe shell call '{match}' — requires explicit security review per AGENTS.md R2"
623	
624	  R3 — Public functions must have docstrings
625	    Pattern: + line matching `def [a-z][^_]` (public, non-dunder) not followed by a + line with """
626	    Reason: "Public function missing docstring per AGENTS.md R3"
627	
628	  R4 — No print() — use efood.logging
629	    Pattern: + line containing "print("
630	    Reason template: "print() call detected — use efood.logging per AGENTS.md R4"
631	
632	  R5 — External HTTP via efood.http_client only
633	    Pattern: + line containing "requests." (get/post/put/delete/patch)
634	    Reason template: "Direct requests.{method} call — use efood.http_client per AGENTS.md R5"
635	
636	(Severities come from _parse_severities(brand), not hardcoded here.)
637	Return one GuardrailCheck per rule, always. If no violation found: result="pass".
638	
639	File: tests/test_guardrails.py
640	
641	  SAMPLE_PATCH (use this for multi-rule tests):
642	    +from .utils import calc
643	    +def apply_discount(order, pct):
644	    +    print(f"Discount applied: {pct}")
645	    +    requests.get(provider_url)
646	
647	  test_r4_print_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R4 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
648	  test_r5_requests_block: SAMPLE_PATCH → R5 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
649	  test_r1_relative_import_warn: SAMPLE_PATCH → R1 result="fail", severity="WARN"
650	  test_clean_patch_all_pass: clean patch (no violations) → all result="pass"
651	  test_r2_subprocess_block: patch with "subprocess.run(" → R2 result="fail", severity="BLOCK"
652	  test_agents_md_drives_severity: modify efood/AGENTS.md R1 from WARN→BLOCK, call
653	    run_checks("+from .x import y", "efood") → R1 severity must be "BLOCK" (proves file is read)
654	    Restore AGENTS.md after test.
655	
656	Done when: uv run pytest tests/test_guardrails.py -v shows all tests passing.
657	```

802	### Design Principles Check (run before JD alignment)
803	
804	| Principle | Verification |
805	|---|---|
806	| SRP — POST /plan response has no `patches` field | `curl .../plan \| python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert 'patches' not in d[0]"` |
807	| SRP — POST /patches response has no `checks` field | Same pattern: `assert 'checks' not in d` |
808	| SRP — LLM input schemas exist and are separate | `grep -n "PlanStepInput\|PatchProposalInput" src/schemas.py` shows two classes |
809	| SRP — Response schemas (Out) exist and are separate | `grep -n "PlanStepOut\|PatchProposalOut" src/schemas.py` shows two classes |
810	| Deterministic Boundary — no LLM call in guardrails.py | `grep -n "anthropic\|client\." src/guardrails.py` returns nothing |
811	| YAGNI — no unspecified features | No SQLite, no auth, no retry loop unless spec asked |
812	| Workflow-first — GET /sessions/{id} shows accumulated state | Full nested state only after all endpoints called in sequence |
813	| Explainability — reason cites rule | `grep "per AGENTS.md" src/guardrails.py` returns one line per rule |
814	
815	**All 10 green = submit. Any red = fix README or add one line of code.**

922	agent workflows — the LLM proposes, deterministic checks decide.
923	It is not a Cursor clone — it is the DH-aware integration layer that injects
924	brand context (AGENTS.md) and enforces Engineering Manifesto guardrails before
925	any AI-generated patch can be merged."
926	
927	AI Leverage section (mandate):
928	"The LLM only performs extraction and proposal generation.
929	Merging, validation, and guardrail evaluation are deterministic."
930	
931	Trade-off (scope control):
932	"I intentionally deferred [X] because [Y] mattered more within the [N]-hour constraint.
933	I would reconsider if [Z]."
934	
935	Multi-brand extensibility (show architectural thinking):
936	"The guardrail loader accepts brand as a parameter.
937	Adding glovo would mean: create glovo/AGENTS.md, add 'glovo' to the Brand literal.
938	No route code changes required."
939	
940	OTEL mention (shows production mindset):
941	"trace_id is generated at session creation and propagated to every GuardrailCheck.
942	In production, these would be exported as OTEL spans to DH OAM."
943	```

946	
947	## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
948	
949	1. **Framework-first** — No LangChain, no LlamaIndex. Direct `anthropic` SDK only.
950	2. **Context dump** — Do NOT load AGENTS.md into a module-level variable at startup. Just-in-time.
951	3. **LLM for guardrails** — R1–R5 are regex. LLM-as-judge is P2, named as such in README.
952	4. **print() anywhere** — Use `logging`. This is also a test case.
953	5. **README last** — Skeleton (all 5 sections + Architecture diagram) before first route.
954	6. **E2E skip** — Pytest green ≠ feature correct. Always run the curl workflow.
955	7. **Guardrail ignores AGENTS.md** — `run_checks(diff, brand)` MUST read `{brand}/AGENTS.md`
956	   and parse severity from it. "brand parameter present but unused" is the failure mode, not
957	   the solution. Litmus test: change a severity in `efood/AGENTS.md` — if the guardrail output
958	   doesn't change, the implementation is wrong.
959	8. **No trace_id** — It is one field, costs nothing, and signals OTEL/OAM awareness from the start.
960	9. **"multi-brand" only in schema** — It must also appear in README as a described extension path.
961	10. **Worktrees not visible in git log** — Merge commits should show feat/routes and feat/guardrails branch names.
962	11. **Severity hardcoded as literals in rule dispatch** — Writing `_check("R1", "WARN", ...)` in
963	    the dispatch list means changing AGENTS.md has no effect. Severity must flow from
964	    `_parse_severities(brand)["R1"]` with a fallback default, never from an inline string literal.
965	
966	---
967	
968	## Production Hardening Tiers
969	
970	Use this when a submission needs to move beyond the in-memory assignment baseline without turning
971	the exercise into infrastructure work.
972	
973	### Tier 1 — Required Before Staging
974	
975	These are must-have implementation requirements. If any are missing, do not call the service
976	production-ready.
977	
978	1. **Persistence**
979	   - Replace module-level `dict[UUID, X]` state with SQLite + SQLModel async persistence.
980	   - Hide storage behind a repository protocol.
981	   - Routes receive storage via `Depends(get_repo)`.
982	
983	2. **Async LLM**
984	   - LLM calls are async end to end.
985	   - Use `AsyncAnthropic` or an equivalent async provider client.
986	   - FastAPI routes and service functions must not block the worker during a long LLM call.
987	
988	3. **Dependency Injection**
989	   - Routes receive `repo`, `settings`, and `llm_client` through FastAPI dependencies.
990	   - Tests override dependencies with `app.dependency_overrides`.
991	   - Avoid service-layer module globals for replaceable infrastructure.
992	
993	4. **Settings**
994	   - Centralize environment configuration in one `pydantic-settings.BaseSettings` class.
995	   - Include `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MODEL`, `USE_VERTEX`, and `DB_URL`.
996	   - Do not scatter `os.getenv()` reads through route/service/LLM code.
997	
998	5. **Centralized Exception Handling**
999	   - Routes should not repeat `try/except HTTPException` wrappers.
1000	   - Register app-level handlers such as `@app.exception_handler(NotFoundError)`.
1001	
1002	### Tier 2 — Keep Narrow Unless Explicitly Asked
1003	
1004	These are operational improvements, but most are over-scope for the take-home. Implement only the
1005	low-cost pieces that protect the core workflow.
1006	
1007	**Implement when hardening the assignment:**
1008	
1009	1. **Authentication + ownership**
1010	   - Use `HTTPBearer` to identify the actor.
1011	   - Store `session.owner_id`.
1012	   - Verify ownership on every session and patch workflow route, including shortcut check endpoints.
1013	
1014	2. **Structured logging + trace context**
1015	   - Use structured logs.
1016	   - Bind `trace_id`, `session_id`, and `actor` consistently before service actions.
1017	
1018	**Usually document as future work unless the user asks:**
1019	
1020	- Full OTEL exporter and custom LLM/DB spans.
1021	- Redis-backed idempotency cache.
1022	- Rate limiting middleware and per-key buckets.
1023	- Real circuit breaker with rolling 5xx thresholds and fail-fast windows.
1024	- Full audit retention policy for prompts/responses.
1025	
1026	### Case-01-Session-Second Baseline
1027	
1028	For `case-01-session-second`, Tier 1 plus the narrow Tier 2 subset above means:
1029	
1030	- SQLite + SQLModel async repository with `Depends(get_repo)`.
1031	- Async LLM wrapper with injectable `LLMProvider` via `Depends(get_llm)`.
1032	- Central `Settings` for `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MODEL`, `USE_VERTEX`, `DB_URL`.
1033	- App-level exception handlers.
1034	- Bearer-token actor stored as `Session.owner_id`.
1035	- Cross-actor access returns 403 for session, plan, patch, and check routes.
1036	- Logs bind `trace_id`, `session_id`, and `actor`.
1037	
1038	Do not add the broader Tier 2 items unless the user explicitly prioritizes production operations
1039	over assignment scope.
1040

1043	## File Output Map
1044	
1045	```
1046	src/
1047	  models.py           ← 도메인 모델 (Session, PlanStep, PatchProposal, GuardrailCheck)
1048	  schemas.py          ← DTOs: 요청(SessionCreate, PatchCreate), LLM I/O(PlanStepInput, PatchProposalInput), 응답(PlanStepOut, PatchProposalOut)
1049	  repository.py       ← SessionRepository(Protocol) — 인터페이스 선언만
1050	  sqlite_repository.py← SQLiteRepository — SQLAlchemy async 구현체
1051	  store.py            ← Phase 3 Agent A (in-memory dict, 초기 구현용)
1052	  llm.py              ← Phase 3 Agent A (just-in-time AGENTS.md + Anthropic call)
1053	  routes.py           ← Phase 3 Agent A (5 FastAPI routes)
1054	  guardrails.py       ← Phase 3 Agent B (regex R1–R5, brand-parametrized)
1055	  auth.py             ← 인증: get_current_actor, ActorDepend (DB 없음)
1056	  guards.py           ← 인가: get_owned_session, verify_patch_ownership (repo 필요)
1057	  deps.py             ← 인프라: get_repo, get_llm, get_settings, IdempotencyContext
1058	  errors.py           ← 도메인 예외 타입 (NotFoundError, OwnershipError) — raise 위치와 분리
1059	  exceptions.py       ← app-level exception handlers (not_found, ownership, llm_unavailable)
1060	  config.py           ← pydantic-settings BaseSettings (AliasChoices 없이 plain 필드)
1061	  main.py             ← Phase 3 Agent A (app factory + /health; registers handlers via add_exception_handler)
1062	tests/
1063	  memory_repository.py← InMemoryRepository — 테스트 전용 (배포 안 되므로 src/ 아님)
1064	  conftest.py         ← DI override: get_repo→InMemoryRepository, get_llm→FakeLLM
1065	  test_guardrails.py  ← Phase 3 Agent B (6 test cases)
1066	  test_e2e.py         ← E2E (TestClient 기반, 실서버 불필요)
1067	{brand}/
1068	  AGENTS.md      ← provided; loaded just-in-time (path: f"{brand}/AGENTS.md")
1069	README.md        ← Phase 1 skeleton, Phase 5 filled
1070	.env.example     ← ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=  (empty = mock mode)
